From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1962 Italian film

| Colpo gobbo all'italiana | |
| --- | --- |

| Directed by | [Lucio Fulci](/wiki/Lucio_Fulci "Lucio Fulci") |
| Screenplay by | * [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") * [Giovanni Grimaldi](/wiki/Giovanni_Grimaldi "Giovanni Grimaldi") |
| Story by | [Mario Carotenuto](/wiki/Mario_Carotenuto "Mario Carotenuto")[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEANICA-1) |
| Cinematography | [Alfio Contini](/wiki/Alfio_Contini "Alfio Contini")[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEANICA-1) |
| Edited by | [Franco Fraticelli](/wiki/Franco_Fraticelli "Franco Fraticelli")[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEANICA-1) |
| Music by | [Piero Umiliani](/wiki/Piero_Umiliani "Piero Umiliani")[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEANICA-1) |
| Production companies | * Mirafilm * Marcus Produzione Cinematografica |
| Release dates | * March 28, 1962 (1962-03-28) ([Turin](/wiki/Turin "Turin") & [Bari](/wiki/Bari "Bari")) |
| Running time | 100 minutes |
| Country | Italy |
| Box office | [ITL](/wiki/Italian_lira "Italian lira") 116.630 million |

***Colpo gobbo all'italiana*** (lit. 'Smart Move Italian-Style') is a 1962 Italian [comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") directed by [Lucio Fulci](/wiki/Lucio_Fulci "Lucio Fulci").[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201861-2)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Colpo_gobbo_all%27italiana&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w.svg) | This article **needs a [plot summary](/wiki/Wikipedia:How_to_write_a_plot_summary "Wikipedia:How to write a plot summary").** Please [add one](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Colpo_gobbo_all%27italiana&action=edit) in your own words. *(January 2026)* |

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Colpo_gobbo_all%27italiana&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Mario Carotenuto](/wiki/Mario_Carotenuto "Mario Carotenuto") as Nando Paciocchi
* [Marisa Merlini](/wiki/Marisa_Merlini "Marisa Merlini") as Nunziata, Maggiola's wife
* [Andrea Checchi](/wiki/Andrea_Checchi "Andrea Checchi") as Orazio Menicotti
* [Gina Rovere](/wiki/Gina_Rovere "Gina Rovere") as Gina, Paciocchi's wife
* [Gino Bramieri](/wiki/Gino_Bramieri "Gino Bramieri") as Panza
* [Aroldo Tieri](/wiki/Aroldo_Tieri "Aroldo Tieri") as Tifillo, the burglar
* [Gabriele Antonini](/wiki/Gabriele_Antonini "Gabriele Antonini") as Ennio
* [Hélène Chanel](/wiki/H%C3%A9l%C3%A8ne_Chanel "Hélène Chanel") as the French blonde
* [Ombretta Colli](/wiki/Ombretta_Colli "Ombretta Colli") as Silvana, Ennio's girlfriend
* [Jole Fierro](/wiki/Jole_Fierro "Jole Fierro") as Ines, a sex worker
* [Nino Terzo](/wiki/Nino_Terzo "Nino Terzo") as Maggiola

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Colpo_gobbo_all%27italiana&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Production")]

*Colpo gobbo all'italiana* was a production of two Italian film production companies: Mirafilm and Marcus Produzione Cinematografica. It was directed by [Lucio Fulci](/wiki/Lucio_Fulci "Lucio Fulci").[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201860-3) While Fulci biographer [Stephen Thrower](/wiki/Stephen_Thrower "Stephen Thrower")'s book *Beyond Terror: The Films of Lucio Fulci* (2018) only credits [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci") and [Giovanni Grimaldi](/wiki/Giovanni_Grimaldi "Giovanni Grimaldi") for the screenplay, [ANICA](/w/index.php?title=ANICA&action=edit&redlink=1 "ANICA (page does not exist)") [[it](https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/ANICA "it:ANICA")] includes [Beppe Costa](/wiki/Beppe_Costa "Beppe Costa").[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEANICA-1)[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201860-3) The film was previously known as *La nottola in bicicletta* (lit. 'The Night Owl on a Bicycle' before its release.[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEB.C.196212-4) The film's opening features a song titled "La Nottola di notte" (lit. 'The Owl of the Night') performed and written by [Gianni Meccia](/wiki/Gianni_Meccia "Gianni Meccia").[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201861-2)

The title *Colpo gobbo all'italiana* combines the names of two other works: *Colpo gobbo* is an Italian title for the [Mickey Spillane](/wiki/Mickey_Spillane "Mickey Spillane")'s novel *[The Big Kill](/wiki/The_Big_Kill "The Big Kill")* (1951) while "all'italiania" borrows from the popular film *[Divorce Italian Style](/wiki/Divorce_Italian_Style "Divorce Italian Style")* (1961).[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower2018105-5)

[Mario Carotenuto](/wiki/Mario_Carotenuto "Mario Carotenuto") was a prolific actor in comedy films of the period in Italy, who previously had appeared in Fulci's earlier films *[Ragazzi del Juke-Box](/wiki/Ragazzi_del_Juke-Box "Ragazzi del Juke-Box")* (1959) and *[Urlatori alla sbarra](/wiki/Urlatori_alla_sbarra "Urlatori alla sbarra")* (1960).[[6]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201859-6) Carotenuto, who had written the story and also served as producer, clashed with Fulci over changes to the script; after filming wrapped, Fulci ended his friendship with him, and the two never worked together again.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Thrower said that the film could be describe as Fulci's contribution to the "oft-reviled" [pink neorealism](/wiki/Pink_neorealism "Pink neorealism") genre of Italian cinema.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201861-2)

## Release and reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Colpo_gobbo_all%27italiana&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Release and reception")]

*Colpo gobbo all'italiana* was distributed in Italy by Mirafilm released to theatres in Turin and [Bari](/wiki/Bari "Bari") May 11, followed by a release in Rome on June 9, 1962.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201860-3) It grossed a total of 116.630 million [Italian lire](/wiki/Italian_lire "Italian lire") in Italy.[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEGraingerThrower2002273-8) The film stayed in theaters through mid-1962 and was still playing in Rome through September. While *Dizionario del cinema italiano* said the box office in Italy was lower than earlier films like *[I ladri](/wiki/I_ladri "I ladri")* (1959), a film Fulci described as a financial flop.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201861-2)

It had a 100-minute runtime on its theatrical release in Italy and a 90-minute runtime in Spain when it played in Barcelona and Seville as *La rubia tuvo la culpa* in 1965.[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEThrower201860-3)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Colpo_gobbo_all%27italiana&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of Italian films of 1962](/wiki/List_of_Italian_films_of_1962 "List of Italian films of 1962")
